eligibility_and_employment_history_quest.explanation_of_above_questions:
I am able to perform the essential duties of a Transfer Station Attendant, including standing and walking for extended periods, lifting and moving items in the 40–50 lb range on a regular basis, and working outdoors in varying weather conditions. I have worked in similar physically demanding roles in warehouses and at a building supply yard, so I am familiar with the physical requirements and with following safety procedures.

I am over 18 and can provide a valid California driver’s license and other standard documentation (such as a Social Security card) to verify my identity and eligibility to work. I am a U.S. citizen, born in Eureka, CA, and my visa or immigration status does not in any way prevent me from lawful employment.

I have not previously worked for Humboldt Waste Management Authority, though I have visited the Hawthorne Street Transfer Station several times as a customer, dropping off household waste and green waste. That experience, plus my background in customer-facing jobs, is part of why I’m interested in this position.

Regarding employment gaps: I do have a brief gap in my work history from approximately 2020-06 to 2020-10. During that period, the small construction company I was working for reduced staff due to COVID-related slowdowns, and I was laid off along with several coworkers. I used that time to help my parents with home repairs, took a short online OSHA safety course, and actively applied for jobs. I returned to full-time work later in 2020 once the economy started to reopen. Aside from that period, my work history has been steady.

I have never been discharged for cause or forced to resign from any job. When I have left positions, it has been either due to seasonal/temporary work ending, better opportunities, or company-wide reductions in staff during the pandemic.

For days missed: in 2018, I missed about 3 days of work due to a bad flu. In 2019, I missed 2 days due to a minor back strain (non-work-related) for which I saw a doctor and was cleared to return with no restrictions. In 2020, I missed about 5 days total—2 days due to a stomach virus and 3 days related to COVID exposure precautions (I stayed home while awaiting test results; I ultimately tested negative). I generally try to schedule any personal appointments outside of work hours or coordinate with my supervisor in advance to minimize impact to the schedule.

I have reliable transportation: a 2012 Toyota Tacoma in good working condition, insured and registered in California. I routinely commute between McKinleyville and Eureka/Arcata and am accustomed to driving in coastal weather conditions. I also have access to public transit as a backup.

I currently hold a valid California Class C driver’s license with no DUIs and no major moving violations. I am willing to provide my driving record if needed and to comply with any additional driving-related policies or trainings required by HWMA.

In summary, I am physically able and willing to perform the job duties, have a stable work history with one explained gap during the early COVID period, and have both the documentation and transportation necessary to be a reliable employee.
